heroPoems.title: If—
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

heroPoems.title: Public Duty of the Wise
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The wise do not need anything,
yet they still act.

heroPoems.title: On the Sea
heroPoems.firstStanza:
It keeps eternal whisperings around
Desolate shores, and with its mighty swell
Gluts twice ten thousand Caverns, till the spell
Of Hecate leaves them their old shadowy sound.
Often 'tis in such gentle temper found,
That scarcely will the very smallest shell

heroPoems.title: Looking for a Sunset Bird in Winter
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The west was getting out of gold,
The breath of air had died of cold,
When shoeing home across the white,
I thought I saw a bird alight.
In summer when I passed the place
I had to stop and lift my face;
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: Two Are Better
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Two are better than one,
because they have good return for their labor.
heroPoems.tagline: For when language keeps you company.

heroPoems.title: On Pain
heroPoems.tagline: For when language keeps you company.

heroPoems.title: Spring will open every branch
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Spring will open every branch.
Flowers will return.
But the children
who fell like last autumn leaves
will not come back.

heroPoems.title: Not all the wealth of earth equals one bowed head in pain
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Not all the wealth of earth equals one bowed head in pain
If I sell my patched cloak for wine
what I receive is greater than what I lose
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: I was not aware of the moment when
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I was not aware of the moment when I first crossed the threshold of
this life.
What was the power that made me open out into this vast mystery like a
bud in the forest at midnight!
When in the morning I looked upon the light I felt in a moment that I
was no stranger in this world, that the inscrutable without name and

heroPoems.title: O servant, where dost thou seek Me?
heroPoems.firstStanza:
O servant, where dost thou seek Me?
Lo!  I am beside thee.
I am neither in temple nor in mosque: I am neither in Kaaba nor
in Kailash:
Neither am I in rites and ceremonies, nor in Yoga and
renunciation.
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

collections.name: After the Last Footstep
collections.tagline: For evenings with no clear ending.
collections.theme: An inward territory of lamplight, weather, and private observation.

featuredPoem.title: Skin and Bones
featuredPoem.firstLines:
My bones cling to my skin.
My days race by.

poems.title: Where is the night, when the sun is shining? If it is night

collections.name: The Hour of Thin Light
collections.theme: Poems that hold small domestic scenes until they become almost sacred.

featuredPoem.title: My house became heaven when she lived in it
featuredPoem.firstLines:
My house became heaven when she lived in it
from head to foot she was grace in motion

poems.title: The Highest Good is Like Water
It was never in my fate to meet you.

collections.name: Where Pages Breathe
collections.tagline: For the quiet after long days.
collections.theme: Language for the suspended hour between conversation and sleep.

featuredPoem.title: Endure the Weather of Mind
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Pleasure and pain pass through.
Do not build a house

poems.title: The Cry from the Ash Heap
The mirror lends me a face and sends me the bill.
